The game¡¯s vast and atmospheric world also provides the foundation for a brand new online multiplayer experience. America, 1899. The end of the Wild West era has begun. After a robbery goes badly wrong in the western town of Blackwater, Arthur Morgan and the Van der Linde gang are forced to flee. With federal agents and the best bounty hunters in the nation massing on their heels, the gang has to rob, steal and fight their way across the rugged heartland of America in order to survive. As deepening internal fissures threaten to tear the gang apart, Arthur must make a choice between his own ideals and loyalty to the gang that raised him. Rankings.",[1474],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1477,"filename":1478,"dateCreated":1479,"alt":1316,"credits":1316,"path":1481,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1482,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"af4c82cd-4a12-4a7e-aef3-e4b48269cfe0","maingamepublishers2026.jpg",{"date":1480,"timezone":1425},"2026-03-24 22:40:55.000000","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2026/03/24/af4c82cd-4a12-4a7e-aef3-e4b48269cfe0/maingamepublishers2026.jpg","\u003Cp>Which game publishers released the best games in 2025?  For the 16th consecutive year, we  sifted through 12 months of  data to determine the best and worst game publishers of the year, based solely on the quality of their 2025 releases. Sales and user reviews do not factor into these rankings; only critic reviews (as captured by each game's Metascore) are used to evaluate performance. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Publishers are ranked from worst to best in the gallery below based on a points system, calculated (as in previous years) according to the following four factors:\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(1) Average Metascore for all  games released in 2025\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>150 possible points (awarded at 1.5 x the average Metascore)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(2) % of scored products with good reviews (Metascore of at least \u003Cspan class=\"fmsgreen\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"75\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-green\">75\u003C/span>)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>100 possible points (ex: 80% good = 80 points)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(3) % of scored products with bad reviews (\u003Cspan class=\"fmsred\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"49\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-red\">49\u003C/span> or lower; in this case, a lower % is better)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>100 possible points (ex: 20% bad = 80 points)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(4) Number of \"great\" titles (Metascore of  \u003Cspan class=\"fmsgreen\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"90\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-green\">90\u003C/span> or higher, min. 7 reviews)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>Awarded as 5 bonus points for each distinct title with a 90+ score\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>As you can see above,  the  Metascore average (the first factor) counts slightly more than the other factors. \u003Cstrong>Only publishers with five or more distinct titles released last year are included in our rankings. \u003C/strong>For fans of fine print, additional notes on our methodology appear at the bottom of this page.\u003C/p>",{"id":1484,"type":1468,"title":1485,"slug":1486,"date":1487,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1488,"tags":1489,"author":1491,"image":1496},"55228829-8f61-4267-9fd0-448b5585cb59","Spring (For  our \u003Cstrong>best\u003C/strong> lists, we have a higher cutoff, but we think  relaxing the requirements here makes for a more fun worst list. We also  opened the list up to mobile and VR releases, DLC, and compilations.) The last new \u003Cem>Metroid\u003C/em> title  was the side-scroller \u003Cem>Metroid Dread\u003C/em> in 2021, the last new 3D \u003Cem>Metroid\u003C/em> game was \u003Cem>The Other M\u003C/em> way back in 2010, and the last \u003Cem>Metroid Prime \u003C/em>dropped  in 2007. But that's all in the past. The question now is whether the first \u003Cem>Metroid  Prime\u003C/em> game in 18 years can live up to the franchise's outsized  legacy.&nbsp;\u003C/p>\u003Cp>So we're taking a look back at the series, ranking most of the past \u003Cem>Metroid\u003C/em> games from worst to best by their Metascores (which represent the views of top game publications), to see  how the new \u003Cem>Metroid Prime 4\u003C/em> stacks up. The first game, \u003Cem>Battlefield 1942\u003C/em>¡ªdeveloped,  like most of its sequels and spinoffs, by Swedish studio  DICE¡ªestablished the popularity of a pretty awesome  subgenre of online  shooter, one in which the maps are enormous and the battles  are filled  with as much vehicular combat as gun fights. The \u003Cem>Battlefield\u003C/em> series is full of shooters  for the thinking person¡ªin this franchise, playing smart matters more than  having excellent aim.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>It's  no wonder, then, that \u003Cem>Battlefield\u003C/em> has  managed to carve itself quite a niche in the gaming market. It's why the new \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/battlefield-6/\">Battlefield 6\u003C/a>\u003Cem>,\u003C/em>  out this Friday, is one of  the most anticipated new video games of  2025. This series offers a ride you  just cannot get from any other  games. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>With \u003Cem>Battlefield 6\u003C/em> arriving this week on  PC, PS5 and Xbox  Series S/X, it's the perfect time to take a look back at the   franchise's 23-year legacy¡ªthat is, how each game in the series, both  mainline entries  and spinoffs alike, have fared with reviewers on  Metacritic. For cross-platform  titles we'll be looking at the version  on the platform with the most reviews.  And we're staying with full  titles for this one¡ªno DLC is included on the list.  So let's take a  look at the \u003Cem>Battlefield\u003C/em> games, ranked from worst to best ...\u003C/p>",{"id":1672,"type":1468,"title":1673,"slug":1674,"date":1675,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1676,"tags":1677,"author":1679,"image":1680},"02354610-c217-4957-b397-73fa183731c5","Every Sucker Punch Productions Game, Ranked","every-sucker-punch-productions-game-ranked-worst-to-best","2025-09-26 17:40:00","We rank every video game developed by Sucker Punch Productions (including the new Ghost of Yotei) from worst to best by Metascore.",[1678],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1492,"id":1493,"lastName":1494,"username":1495},{"id":1681,"filename":1682,"dateCreated":1683,"alt":1316,"credits":1685,"path":1686,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1687,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"9c6a831f-65b2-49b7-adbc-f7e99bd0710c","mainsuckerpunch.jpg",{"date":1684,"timezone":1425},"2025-09-26 17:09:51.000000","\"Ghost of Y¨­tei\" (Sony Interactive)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/09/26/9c6a831f-65b2-49b7-adbc-f7e99bd0710c/mainsuckerpunch.jpg","\u003Cp>Over the past  three decades, Sucker Punch Productions has been one  of gaming's most reliable  development studios, churning out hit after  hit for PlayStation for 25 years  without ever producing \u003Cem>anything\u003C/em>  that  critics have broadly disliked. Sucker Punch has as good a track  record as any  studio that's been around as long as they have. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Over the years,  Sucker Punch has been responsible for creating three beloved PlayStation  franchises, starting with \u003Cem>Sly Cooper\u003C/em> on the PS2, \u003Cem>inFamous\u003C/em> on the PS3, and \u003Cem>Ghost of Tsushima\u003C/em>  on the PS4. That last  one proved to be Sucker Punch's biggest hit yet,  and so it only makes sense  that the company's follow-up would be a  bigger and better sequel. And that new game,  \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/ghost-of-yotei/\">Ghost of Yotei\u003C/a>, has already inspired a lot  of very enthusiastic reviews ahead of its launch on October 2.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>We're marking the  release of \u003Cem>Ghost of Yotei\u003C/em> by looking   back at reviews for Sucker Punch's past games, so we can see how the new  game  stacks up against the company's long history of quality titles.  Unfortunately,  we don't have enough reviews to include Sucker Punch's  very first game, \u003Cem>Rocket: Robot on Wheels\u003C/em> for the Nintendo  64, so it's not included in this list.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Let's dive in!\u003C/p>",{"id":1689,"type":1468,"title":1690,"slug":1691,"date":1692,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1693,"tags":1694,"author":1696,"image":1697},"d20d3298-8c11-484f-bbf9-a5a3a6896456","Every Borderlands Game, Ranked","every-borderlands-game-ranked-worst-to-best","2025-09-11 16:18:00","We rank every main and spinoff game in the Borderlands series, including the newest game, Borderlands 4.",[1695],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1492,"id":1493,"lastName":1494,"username":1495},{"id":1698,"filename":1699,"dateCreated":1700,"alt":1316,"credits":1702,"path":1703,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1704,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"ad852186-661d-4652-bf15-cadd8b2728bd","mainborderlands.jpg",{"date":1701,"timezone":1425},"2025-09-11 16:19:48.000000","\"Borderlands 4\" (2K Games/Gearbox)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/09/11/ad852186-661d-4652-bf15-cadd8b2728bd/mainborderlands.jpg","\u003Cp>Since the first  game landed on store shelves back in 2009, the \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em>  franchise has become both massively popular among  players and  massively influential in the video games industry. With its mashup  of  the shooter and RPG genres, its expansive randomized loot system, its   irreverent sense of humor, \u003Cem>and\u003C/em> the  fact that you could play the whole thing with up to three of your friends, \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em> helped usher in a new era  for gaming. Many other games have borrowed the \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em> blueprint over the past two decades, but in 2025 you're  only going to get the real thing from one place: \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/borderlands-4/\">Borderlands 4\u003C/a>,  which arrives in digital and physical stores on  September 12 for Xbox  Series X/S, PlayStation 5, and PC, and October 3 on Switch  2.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Despite the  franchise's popularity, the folks at \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em>  creator Gearbox haven't tried to exploit it by churning out yearly new  games,  or by turning its formula into a live-service affair with a  seasonal battle  pass grind the way Bungie did with \u003Cem>Destiny\u003C/em>. What it \u003Cem>has\u003C/em> done, however, is branch out with other types of games that  explore other corners of \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em>'  unique sci-fi universe. So even though we're only on the fifth mainline game in  the franchise¡ªyes, \u003Cem>Borderlands 4\u003C/em>  is  the fifth game¡ªthere have also been several spinoffs and smaller  releases that  have expanded the series in fascinating ways. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>We're marking the  launch of \u003Cem>Borderlands 4\u003C/em> by looking  back at how well the various games of the \u003Cem>Borderlands\u003C/em>  franchise have gone down with reviewers on Metacritic, including its  spinoffs  and one particularly important piece of DLC that spawned its  own little  sub-franchise. We'll be using the Metascore from the  platform receiving the most reviews for each title, and the list won't  include mobile-only  games. Let's dive in.\u003C/p>",{"id":1706,"type":1468,"title":1707,"slug":1708,"date":1709,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1710,"tags":1711,"author":1713,"image":1714},"2ba0ab25-28ef-4d65-a537-9de070ff99ec","Every Metal Gear Video Game, Ranked","every-metal-gear-video-game-ranked-worst-to-best","2025-08-22 15:53:00","We rank every game in the Metal Gear series, including the new Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater, from worst to best by Metascore.",[1712],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1492,"id":1493,"lastName":1494,"username":1495},{"id":1715,"filename":1716,"dateCreated":1717,"alt":1316,"credits":1719,"path":1720,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1721,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"4ff6da27-dec7-47f9-8410-697f08b24876","mainmetalgear.jpg",{"date":1718,"timezone":1425},"2025-08-21 23:01:29.000000","\"Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain\" (Konami)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/08/21/4ff6da27-dec7-47f9-8410-697f08b24876/mainmetalgear.jpg","\u003Cp>There aren't many video game franchises that are as well-liked or  long-running as \u003Cem>Metal Gear\u003C/em>, which made its debut on the MSX personal  computer all the way back in 1987. Since then, \u003Cem>Metal Gear\u003C/em>  and its creator Hideo  Kojima have been institutions in the gaming  space¡ªthough the franchise has  fallen off a bit in recent years since  it hasn't had a new game or re-release  since 2018. But that's about the  change with the August 28 release of \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/metal-gear-solid-delta-snake-eater/\">Metal  Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater\u003C/a>, Konami's  modern remake of 2004's \u003Cem>Metal  Gear Solid 3\u003C/em>.&nbsp;\u003C/p>\u003Cp>The imminent arrival of \u003Cem>Metal Gear Solid Delta\u003C/em> makes this   the perfect time to look back on how critics have responded over the  years to  the many games this series has given us. Those games  have  frequently entranced  players and critics alike with their enchanting  combination of compelling  stealth gameplay and in-depth storytelling  that we rarely used to see in video  games. We have ranked just about  all of the games in franchise history below by each title's Metascore,  which represents the consensus of top professional critics on a scale of  0 (worst) to 100 (best). \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Unfortunately, we're not able to check the reviews for \u003Cem>every\u003C/em> game in the franchise, as the three earliest titles, \u003Cem>Metal Gear\u003C/em> (1987), \u003Cem>Snake's Revenge\u003C/em> (1990), and \u003Cem>Metal Gear 2: Solid Snake\u003C/em>  (1990),  came out well before Metacritic existed¡ªand before anyone was   publishing reviews online, really. For the sake of thoroughness, we're  including most of the  different editions of each of the games, since  Konami has long been fond of  overhauling and re-releasing these games  with new features. \u003Cem>MGS Delta\u003C/em>,  for example, will be the fourth new version of \u003Cem>Snake Eater\u003C/em>, so those  updated editions are major parts of the franchise's lore. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>For games that were released on multiple platforms, the first  version released is the one eligible for this list. If the game was  released on multiple platforms simultaneously, the version receiving the  largest number of reviews from critics was eligible.  Mobile phone  games, DLC, and multi-game compilations are excluded. With that in mind,   let's dive in.\u003C/p>",{"id":1723,"type":1468,"title":1724,"slug":1725,"date":1726,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1727,"tags":1728,"author":1730,"image":1731},"b38c3613-6450-40b9-aa14-ebe1863969aa","Every Nintendo Franchise, Ranked","every-nintendo-franchise-ranked-worst-to-best","2025-07-31 23:44:00","We rank every Nintendo game franchise in history from worst to best based on their average Metascores.",[1729],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1732,"filename":1733,"dateCreated":1734,"alt":1316,"credits":1736,"path":1737,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1738,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"44f9caf4-2ce4-4fc9-be03-b412af63343a","mainnintendofranchises.jpg",{"date":1735,"timezone":1425},"2025-07-31 23:01:07.000000","\"Mario Kart 8 Deluxe\" (Nintendo)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/07/31/44f9caf4-2ce4-4fc9-be03-b412af63343a/mainnintendofranchises.jpg","\u003Cp>With the wildly successful launch of the Switch 2 earlier this year,  Nintendo has once again reinforced its status as perhaps the preeminent  gaming hardware company of all time. But what has enabled that  multi-decade hardware success has been software: More than any other  console manufacturer, Nintendo has long depended on exclusive,  first-party titles to drive its hardware success, many showcasing the  creativity, playfulness, and humor that Nintendo is known for. The  result is that Nintendo has some of the most lucrative game franchises  of all time, led by its iconic Mario and  Legend of Zelda properties. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Which of those franchises have consistently impressed game critics,  and which have failed to delight? Below, we rank every  Nintendo-published game franchise from worst to best by the average  Metascore for each franchise.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Some notes on our methodology:\u003C/p>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>Only franchises with at least 4 scored games were eligible.\u003C/li>\u003Cli>For  the biggest franchises, like Mario, we divided them into  smaller lists of subseries (for Mario, that means separate listings for  Super Mario, Paper Mario, Mario Party, etc.).\u003C/li>\u003Cli>Though remakes and ports are included below, we excluded DLC,  most multi-game compilations, games on non-Nintendo  hardware (i.e., iOS  games),  arcade  and Game &amp; Watch games (which we don't have scores  for),  and  Japan-only releases.\u003C/li>\u003Cli>For some older pre-Metacritic titles for which we do not have  Metascores, we substituted scores from our now-defunct sister site  GameRankings, which used a similar 0-100 scoring system. (Those scores  are marked with an asterisk below.)\u003C/li>\u003Cli>Release years listed below reflect North American release dates. (Older titles were often released earlier in Japan.)\u003C/li>\u003C/ul>",{"id":1740,"type":1468,"title":1741,"slug":1742,"date":1743,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1744,"tags":1745,"author":1747,"image":1748},"0f55c948-a29e-4112-ae9d-2a425c9149f6","The Best Video Games of 2025 So Far","best-videogames-of-2025-at-midyear","2025-06-27 15:58:00","We reveal the 20 best-reviewed video games (for all platforms) released in the first half of 2025, ranked by Metascore.",[1746],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1749,"filename":1750,"dateCreated":1751,"alt":1316,"credits":1753,"path":1754,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1755,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"57adf068-e153-4768-a795-e13bac602783","2025-games-midyear-clairobscur.jpg",{"date":1752,"timezone":1425},"2025-06-27 15:59:48.000000","\"Clair Obscur: Expedition 33\" (Kepler Interactive/Sandfall Interactive SAS)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/06/27/57adf068-e153-4768-a795-e13bac602783/2025-games-midyear-clairobscur.jpg","\u003Cp>Which video games  impressed critics  during the first half of the  year? On this page we rank the highest-scoring games with release dates  between January 1, 2025 and June 30, 2025. Titles are included based on  their Metascores as of June 25, 2025 and must have a \u003Cstrong>minimum of 7 reviews\u003C/strong> from professional critics to be eligible for inclusion.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Note that ... \u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cstrong>If a game was released on multiple platforms, we included only the version receiving the largest total number of reviews\u003C/strong> (usually, but not always, the PS5  version). \u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cstrong>Newly released ports of  games released in prior years on other platforms are  excluded\u003C/strong>,  unless they are significantly different from the prior release(s) (for  example, an HD remaster  that also adds some new content or is multiple  hardware generations beyond the previous release). \u003Cstrong>Expansions/DLC, mobile games, compilations, and \"Definitive\"/GOTY editions are  also ineligible\u003C/strong>. \u003C/p>",{"id":1757,"type":1404,"title":1758,"slug":1759,"date":1760,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1761,"tags":1762,"author":1764,"image":1765},"e20e6087-c931-4a60-9503-a8d96f5089f7","Hardware Review: Nintendo Switch 2","nintendo-switch-2-console-hardware-review","2025-06-06 15:54:00","Is Nintendo's first new console in eight years worth a purchase at its very steep price? 18:40:33.000000","\"Super Mario Odyssey\" (Nintendo)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/05/27/d883083d-bcee-40d8-9c54-4d5835913d4d/mainswitchexclusives.jpg","\u003Cp>Coming off  the \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/browse/game/wii-u/all/all-time/metascore/?releaseYearMin=1958&amp;releaseYearMax=2025&amp;platform=wii-u&amp;page=1\">disappointing Wii U era\u003C/a>,  Nintendo needed a win in a big way¡ªand it got it  with the Switch  console, which has become Nintendo's best-selling piece of  hardware \u003Cem>ever\u003C/em>.  But after eight  years the reign of the Nintendo Switch is coming to an  end as the company  prepares to launch its successor, the Switch 2, in  early June. So now is the right time to reflect  on the games that  helped make the Nintendo Switch such a massive success.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>While it  would be easy to simply credit the Switch's success to its  unique form factor¡ªthat it works both as a console for your living room  and as a portable you can  take with you¡ªit's the system's \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/browse/game/nintendo-switch/all/all-time/metascore/?releaseYearMin=1958&amp;releaseYearMax=2025&amp;platform=nintendo-switch&amp;page=1\">strong lineup of games\u003C/a>,  in particular its  exclusives, that put the Switch over the top. Note that  only games with at least  seven reviews were eligible for inclusion  here.\u003C/p>",{"id":1790,"type":1468,"title":1791,"slug":1792,"date":1793,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1794,"tags":1795,"author":1797,"image":1798},"be9e7a35-b52b-4d44-a969-87b076bed952","Every Assassin's Creed Game, Ranked","every-assassins-creed-game-ranked-worst-to-best","2025-03-18 17:02:00","We rank every game in the Assassin's Creed franchise¡ªincluding this week's new game Assassin's Creed Shadows¡ªfrom worst to best based on Metascore.",[1796],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1492,"id":1493,"lastName":1494,"username":1495},{"id":1799,"filename":1800,"dateCreated":1801,"alt":1316,"credits":1803,"path":1804,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1805,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"4849f134-36d7-4c75-920c-c3d9829e88af","mainassassinscreedgames.jpg",{"date":1802,"timezone":1425},"2025-03-17 22:40:23.000000","\"Assassin's Creed Mirage\" (Ubisoft)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/03/17/4849f134-36d7-4c75-920c-c3d9829e88af/mainassassinscreedgames.jpg","\u003Cp>Since it  originally launched  in 2007, the \u003Cem>Assassin's  Creed\u003C/em>  franchise has become a gaming institution, with more than a dozen   mainline titles in the series along with a big pile of spinoffs, mobile  titles,  novels, comic books, and even a \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/movie/assassins-creed/\">live-action movie\u003C/a> starring Michael Fassbender.  It was \u003Cem>Assassin's Creed\u003C/em>  that proved  to the  gaming industry that historical action games could  sell just as  well as fantasy or sci-fi titles, paving the way for  recent hits like \u003Cem>Ghost of Tsushima\u003C/em> and \u003Cem>Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2\u003C/em>.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>But it's not  as though \u003Cem>Assassin's Creed\u003C/em> is an  also-ran. Ubisoft's flagship series lives on in 2025 with \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/assassins-creed-shadows/\">Assassin's Creed Shadows\u003C/a>, the 14th mainline title in the series. \u003Cem>Shadows\u003C/em>  takes players through a new story in the never-ending conflict  between  the Assassin Brotherhood and the Templar Order in 16th century Japan. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>As we gear up for the franchise's latest adventure across time, let's take a  look back at how well that title and past \u003Cem>Assassin's Creed\u003C/em>  games have fared with critics.  Below you'll find all 14 mainline  titles ranked by Metascore, starting with the  worst-reviewed game and  working our way up to the best.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cem>Note that we are only ranking  the  original releases for mainline \u003C/em>Assassin's Creed\u003Cem> titles. Spinoffs, remasters, or games that are exclusive  to mobile or VR are excluded. Since every mainline \u003C/em>Assassin's Creed\u003Cem> game is a multiplatform affair, we'll be using the Metascore for the  platform with the most critic reviews for each title.\u003C/em>\u003C/p>",{"id":1807,"type":1468,"title":1808,"slug":1809,"date":1810,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1811,"tags":1812,"author":1814,"image":1815},"5c684b37-4c0d-427d-999c-90f851e7a6dd","Metacritic's 15th Annual Game Publisher Rankings","2025-game-publisher-rankings","2025-03-05 16:00:00","We reveal the past year's best and worst video game publishers (based on their 2024 releases) in the 15th edition of our annual Game Publisher Rankings.",[1813],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1816,"filename":1817,"dateCreated":1818,"alt":1316,"credits":1316,"path":1820,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1821,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"51b7aabb-1c36-4598-9586-3c985689a5e4","landinggamepublisherrankings2025rev.jpg",{"date":1819,"timezone":1425},"2025-03-05 00:14:23.000000","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/03/05/51b7aabb-1c36-4598-9586-3c985689a5e4/landinggamepublisherrankings2025rev.jpg","\u003Cp>Which game publishers released the best games in 2024?  For the 15th  consecutive year, we  sifted through 12 months of  data to determine the  best and worst game publishers of the year, based solely on the quality  of their 2024 releases. Sales and user reviews do not factor into these  rankings; only critic reviews (as captured by each game's Metascore)  are used to evaluate performance. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Publishers are ranked from worst to best in the gallery below  based on a points system, calculated (as in previous years) according to  the following four factors:\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(1) Average Metascore for all  games released in 2024\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>150 possible points (awarded at 1.5 x the average Metascore)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(2) % of scored products with good reviews (Metascore of at least \u003Cspan class=\"fmsgreen\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"75\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-green\">75\u003C/span>)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>100 possible points (ex: 80% good = 80 points)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(3) % of scored products with bad reviews (\u003Cspan class=\"fmsred\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"49\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-red\">49\u003C/span> or lower; in this case, a lower % is better)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>100 possible points (ex: 20% bad = 80 points)\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>(4) Number of \"great\" titles (Metascore of  \u003Cspan class=\"fmsgreen\" data-shortcode=\"textscore\" data-score=\"90\" data-classnames=\"o-inlineScore-green\">90\u003C/span> or higher, min. 7 reviews)\u003Cbr>       \u003Cem>Awarded as 5 bonus points for each distinct title with a 90+ score\u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>As you can see above,  the  Metascore average (the first factor) counts slightly more than the other factors. \u003Cstrong>Only publishers with five or more distinct titles released last year are included in our rankings. \u003C/strong>For fans of fine print, additional notes on our methodology appear at the bottom of this page.\u003C/p>",{"id":1823,"type":1468,"title":1824,"slug":1825,"date":1826,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1827,"tags":1828,"author":1830,"image":1831},"24fd2873-4515-46fb-9d03-dd2b282298ef","Every Like a Dragon/Yakuza Game, Ranked","every-like-a-dragon-yakuza-game-ranked","2025-02-18 16:42:00","(Updated February 2026) We rank every English-language release in the Like a Dragon [aka Yakuza] videogame series from worst to best by Metascore.",[1829],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1492,"id":1493,"lastName":1494,"username":1495},{"id":1832,"filename":1833,"dateCreated":1834,"alt":1316,"credits":1836,"path":1837,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1838,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"c2db7015-f36f-4c0b-868b-8d7f93a2175a","mainlikeadragongames.jpg",{"date":1835,"timezone":1425},"2025-02-14 23:58:56.000000","\"Like a Dragon: Pirate Yakuza in Hawaii\" (Sega)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/02/14/c2db7015-f36f-4c0b-868b-8d7f93a2175a/mainlikeadragongames.jpg","\u003Cp>The \u003Cem>Yakuza\u003C/em> video game series used to be very  niche. These  were games that, in the early years of the franchise, did well in   Japan, but didn't make a huge impression in the West. But that has   changed in a  very big way in  recent years. Sega has put more effort  into localizing  these games, and players have responded by turning \u003Cem>Yakuza\u003C/em>¡ªlater renamed \u003Cem>Like a Dragon\u003C/em>  in the West to match its Japanese title¡ªinto a celebrated  modern  franchise, culminating in the outlandish new entry in the series, \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/like-a-dragon-pirate-yakuza-in-hawaii/\">Like a Dragon: Pirate Yakuza in Hawaii\u003C/a>.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>As you'd  expect from a title like \"Yakuza,\"  these games are  sprawling and melodramatic single-player Japanese crime dramas  in which  you'll have to unravel some big mystery or conspiracy that involves   somebody¡ªusually a rival clan, rivals in your own clan, or government   agents¡ªgetting overly ambitious and making a major power play. And your  character,  typically  a gangster with a heart of gold, is the only one  who can  stop whatever it is. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>But it's  really not the melodrama that's kept people playing the  Yakuza series all these  years. On top of the main plot, the big appeal  of the franchise is that when  you're in between story beats, you get to  hang out in Japan. These are open  world games that provide access to a  number of different locations  where you can just go to bars and  restaurants, go on dates, play arcade games,  get mugged, engage in  random hijinks with strangers, and so much more. You don't  just play  these games¡ªyou kinda live in 'em. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Right now,  we're celebrating this week's arrival of \u003Cem> \u003C/em> \u003Cem>Like a Dragon: Pirate Yakuza in  Hawaii\u003C/em>   by looking back on this lengthy franchise and ranking every key   entry, by Metascore, from worst to best. Note that if a title was  released on multiple  platforms, \u003Cstrong>only the version receiving  the highest quantity of reviews from professional critics\u003C/strong>  was eligible for  inclusion. We have included major remakes as well as  spinoffs set in the same  world but excluded any titles that were never  released in North America.\u003C/p>",{"id":1840,"type":1468,"title":1841,"slug":1842,"date":1843,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1844,"tags":1845,"author":1847,"image":1848},"d77c6f46-e8e3-4023-af1b-b7990ac137b6","Every Civilization Game, Ranked","every-sid-meiers-civilization-game-ranked","2025-02-05 01:18:00","Itching to play just one more turn? With this week's arrival of Civilization 7, we rank every release in the Sid Meier's Civilization turn-based strategy game franchise (including spin-offs) from worst to best by Metascore.",[1846],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1849,"filename":1850,"dateCreated":1851,"alt":1316,"credits":1853,"path":1854,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1855,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"8c8fe97c-cdda-4e07-8d21-5834c5dbae69","maincivilizationgamesranked.jpg",{"date":1852,"timezone":1425},"2025-02-05 00:48:41.000000","Gilgamesh will remember this. (\"Civilization VI,\" Firaxis Games/2K Games)","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/02/05/8c8fe97c-cdda-4e07-8d21-5834c5dbae69/maincivilizationgamesranked.jpg","\u003Cp>No game franchise triggers the \"just one more turn\" syndrome more than  Sid Meier's \u003Cem>Civilization\u003C/em>¡ªin  fact, that phrase is even baked right into recent entries in the  series. Though not the first turn-based, 4X (\"eXplore\" / \"eXpand\" /  \"eXploit\" / \"eXterminate\") strategy franchise to arrive on the market,  1991's \u003Cem>Civilization\u003C/em> and its  successors quickly established themselves as the 4X games against which all others are measured, thanks to \u003Cem>Civ\u003C/em>'s hugely addictive gameplay loop and depth. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>That franchise expands this week with the arrival of \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/sid-meiers-civilization-vii/\">Civilization VII\u003C/a>,  the first all-new entry in the series in nearly a decade. How does it  stack up to the previous titles in a series that has sold more than 70  million units and frequently ranks among the greatest game franchises of  all time? To find out, we have ranked every \u003Cem>Civilization\u003C/em> game  from worst to best. Those rankings are determined by each game's  Metascore, a number from 0-100 that represents the overall consensus  from the most respected game publications and professional critics. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Unfortunately, the original \u003Cem>Civilization\u003C/em> game is excluded  from our list, as its 1991 release date (which predates both Metacritic  and its one-time sister site GameRankings) means that we don't have  enough reviews available to allow us to calculate a score. That game,  developed by Sid Meier and his then-company MicroProse and loosely  inspired by a 1980 Avalon Hill board game of the same name, established  the basic gameplay template for the series¡ªincluding the then-novel  technology tree and an epic scope that spans millennia¡ªthat persists to  this day, even if the implementation and complexity have changed  considerably. And Meier's name has been included as part of the official  title of nearly every release, with most subsequent games developed by  the Meier-founded studio Firaxis (now owned by Take-Two Interactive). \u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cstrong>Each title is only included once below even if was released for multiple platforms. \u003C/strong>Unless  otherwise indicated, only  the original PC version of each game was  eligible to be ranked here (though many of the titles were later ported  to consoles). The exception is games released only on consoles, in which  case the version receiving the highest quantity of critic reviews is  the one included below. \u003Cstrong>In addition to the main numbered series, we have also included spin-off games\u003C/strong> (with the exceptions of 1994's \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/sid-meiers-colonization/\">Sid Meier's Colonization\u003C/a>, 1999's \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/civilization-call-to-power/\">Civilization: Call to Power\u003C/a>, and the 2011 Facebook game \u003Cem>Civilization World\u003C/em>, for which no reviews are available) \u003Cstrong>but not DLC expansions\u003C/strong>.\u003C/p>",{"id":1857,"type":1468,"title":1858,"slug":1859,"date":1860,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1861,"tags":1862,"author":1864,"image":1865},"93715289-8f02-41e2-a6c6-2384751d0e4f","The 20 Best PlayStation Games of 2024","best-playstation-games-of-2024","2025-01-03 17:24:00","We rank the highest-scoring new PlayStation games released in 2024.",[1863],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1866,"filename":1867,"dateCreated":1868,"alt":1316,"credits":1316,"path":1870,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1871,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"fd17bd10-2a60-4383-b918-17e5d91d67ad","mainbestgames2024playstation.jpg",{"date":1869,"timezone":1425},"2025-01-03 00:28:30.000000","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/01/03/fd17bd10-2a60-4383-b918-17e5d91d67ad/mainbestgames2024playstation.jpg","\u003Cp>Which PlayStation games  impressed professional reviewers the most  during the past year? (For  our \u003Cstrong>best\u003C/strong> lists, we have a higher cutoff, but we think  relaxing the requirements here makes for a more fun worst list. We also  opened the list up to mobile and VR releases, DLC, and compilations.) Find out what critics are saying about the PS5 Pro.",[1958],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":1961,"filename":1962,"dateCreated":1963,"alt":1316,"credits":1965,"path":1966,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1428,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"23f5ad73-0023-45c4-a756-fd1a3f6adf22","playstation5proconsole.jpg",{"date":1964,"timezone":1425},"2024-11-01 23:26:34.000000","Sony Interactive Entertainment","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2024/11/01/23f5ad73-0023-45c4-a756-fd1a3f6adf22/playstation5proconsole.jpg",{"id":1968,"type":1468,"title":1969,"slug":1970,"date":1971,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1972,"tags":1973,"author":1975,"image":1980},"adb809c3-4019-4664-9569-4e8c80a6151c","The 20 Best Original Games of the 21st Century","best-original-games-of-the-21st-century","2024-11-05 22:27:00","We rank the highest-scoring \"original\" games (that aren't sequels or remakes or based on existing IP) released since January 1, 2000.",[1974],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1976,"id":1977,"lastName":1978,"username":1979},"Nick","7974d639-0d7a-4f9d-bc19-92d8e0ac3199","Hyman","nickhyman",{"id":1981,"filename":1982,"dateCreated":1983,"alt":1316,"credits":1985,"path":1986,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1987,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"316e1b05-16c9-4054-a80c-f04b548e019a","mainbestoriginalgames.jpg",{"date":1984,"timezone":1425},"2024-11-05 22:28:28.000000","FromSoftware/Bandai Namco/Metacritic","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2024/11/05/316e1b05-16c9-4054-a80c-f04b548e019a/mainbestoriginalgames.jpg","\u003Cp>\u003Cem>They aren't sequels. They aren't spinoffs. They aren't remakes.  They aren't based on any pre-existing IP. Yes, it's true: The video game  industry sometimes produces completely original games. Below, we reveal  the very best of these original games released since January 1, 2000 as  ranked by their Metascores, a number from 0 to 100 that represents the  overall views of top professional game critics. (The higher the number,  the better the reviews.) \u003C/em>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cem>Note that if a title was released on multiple platforms at  roughly the same time, only the version receiving the highest quantity  of reviews was eligible for inclusion. And if a game was later ported to  one or more additional consoles, only the first release was eligible.\u003C/em>\u003C/p>",{"id":1989,"type":1468,"title":1990,"slug":1991,"date":1992,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":1993,"tags":1994,"author":1996,"image":1997},"da7f5062-7c30-42e8-ab10-a5cb400cda12","Every Silent Hill Game, Ranked","every-silent-hill-game-ranked-worst-to-best","2024-10-04 15:40:00","(Updated September 2025) We rank every video game in Konami's Silent Hill franchise from worst to best by Metascore.",[1995],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1316,"firstName":1976,"id":1977,"lastName":1978,"username":1979},{"id":1998,"filename":1999,"dateCreated":2000,"alt":1316,"credits":2002,"path":2003,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":2004,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"68798557-b481-4653-9506-cb11a232de0b","mainsilenthillgamesrankedrev.jpg",{"date":2001,"timezone":1425},"2025-09-22 17:54:42.000000","Konami / Metacritic","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2025/09/22/68798557-b481-4653-9506-cb11a232de0b/mainsilenthillgamesrankedrev.jpg","\u003Cp>When you hear the term \"survival horror\" in the video game  realm, \u003Cem>Resident Evil \u003C/em>and \u003Cem>Silent Hill \u003C/em>are likely the first two  franchises that come to mind. Throughout the years the Konami-developed \u003Cem>Silent Hill\u003C/em>  series, which started as atmospheric small-town mysteries that get  increasingly freaky  as you progress, has mutated outside of just games  with two movies (\u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/movie/silent-hill/\">Silent Hill\u003C/a>, \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/movie/silent-hill-revelation-3d/\">Silent  Hill: Revelation\u003C/a>), books, pachinko machines, and even an appearance of the  dreaded Pyramid Head in the newly released \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/astro-bot/\">Astro Bot\u003C/a>. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>This month,  the franchise  is celebrating 25 years with a remake of one of the more  acclaimed titles in the series, \u003Cem>Silent Hill 2\u003C/em>. To celebrate that game's arrival, let's turn the  lights off and take a mysterious journey through the \u003Cem>Silent Hill\u003C/em>  video  game series as we rank each entry based on their Metascores,  representing the critical reaction to the games upon initial release   (with higher numbers on our 0-100 scale equating to better overall   reviews from top professional critics).&nbsp;\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Smartphone games, multi-game compilations, and repackages of existing  games (such as \"Director's Cut\" editions) have been excluded from the  list. And note that when a game has been released on multiple platforms,  we are only including the score for the platform that received the  largest quantity of reviews from professional critics. \u003C/p>",{"id":2006,"type":1468,"title":2007,"slug":2008,"date":2009,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":2010,"tags":2011,"author":2013,"image":2014},"9264e137-127f-41e1-a040-a7e59d58dfb1","The Best Video Games of 2024 So Far","best-video-games-of-2024-at-midyear","2024-06-27 15:47:00","We rank the highest-scoring new video games released during the first half of 2024.",[2012],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":2015,"filename":2016,"dateCreated":2017,"alt":1316,"credits":2019,"path":2020,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":2021,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"8a6d4853-10d9-4cdb-abea-8893c6635d30","mainbestgames2024midyear.jpg",{"date":2018,"timezone":1425},"2024-06-27 15:47:21.000000","Square Enix / Metacritic","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2024/06/27/8a6d4853-10d9-4cdb-abea-8893c6635d30/mainbestgames2024midyear.jpg","\u003Cp>Which videogames  impressed critics  during the first half of the  year? On this page we rank the highest-scoring games with release dates  between January 1, 2024 and June 30, 2024. Titles are included based on  their Metascores as of June 26, 2024 and must have a \u003Cstrong>minimum of 7 reviews\u003C/strong> from professional critics to be eligible for inclusion.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>Note that ... \u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cstrong>If a game was released on multiple platforms, we included only the version receiving the largest total number of reviews\u003C/strong> (usually, but not always, the PS5 or PS4 version). \u003C/p>\u003Cp>\u003Cstrong>Multi-game compilations and newly released ports of  games released in recent years on other platforms are  excluded\u003C/strong>,  unless they are substantially different from the prior release(s) (for  example, an HD remaster  that also adds some new content or is multiple  hardware generations beyond the previous release). \u003Cstrong>Mobile games, \"Definitive\"/GOTY editions, and expansions/DLC are  also ineligible\u003C/strong>. (Yes, that means you won't find \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/elden-ring-shadow-of-the-erdtree/\">Shadow of the Erdtree\u003C/a> here.)\u003C/p>",{"id":2023,"type":1468,"title":2024,"slug":2025,"date":2026,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":2027,"tags":2028,"author":2030,"image":2031},"5049c2c8-77ee-42a8-b0b3-12ba41157406","Every Metacritic Game of the Year Winner","every-metacritic-game-of-the-year-winner","2024-06-11 00:00:00","(Updated January 2026) These are the best-reviewed games released each year in the 21st century.",[2029],{"id":1411,"name":1412,"slug":1413},{"byline":1415,"firstName":1416,"id":1417,"lastName":1418,"username":1419},{"id":2032,"filename":2033,"dateCreated":2034,"alt":1316,"credits":2036,"path":2037,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":2038,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1447,"height":1448,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1316,"bucketPath":1316,"mediaType":1316},"2f578434-0805-461f-abbc-e65995e261dd","landinggameoftheyearwinners.jpg",{"date":2035,"timezone":1425},"2024-06-10 23:35:49.000000","Metacritic / Rockstar Games","https://www.metacritic.com/a/img/2024/06/10/2f578434-0805-461f-abbc-e65995e261dd/landinggameoftheyearwinners.jpg","\u003Cp>What were the best-reviewed games in every year in the 21st century?  Below are each of Metacritic's Game of the Year honorees for every year  since our launch in 2000, listed in reverse chronological order.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>What makes a game  a Metacritic Game of the Year recipient?  Basically, that title is given to the highest-scoring new game released  in each calendar year, with these qualifications:\u003C/p>\u003Cul>\u003Cli>The Game of the Year recipient has the highest Metascore (a number  from 0-100 representing the consensus opinion of top game review  publications), prior to rounding, for all games released in a calendar  year.\u003C/li>\u003Cli>Games \u003Cstrong>must have at least 7 reviews\u003C/strong> from professional critics to be eligible for inclusion\u003C/li>\u003Cli>\u003Cstrong>For titles released  on multiple platforms, only the version receiving the largest total number of reviews is eligible\u003C/strong>.\u003C/li>\u003Cli>It must be a \"new\" game: If the same title was released the  previous year for other platforms, it does not qualify again in the  second year (each title can only be eligible in its first year).  However, remakes/remasters/ports are considered  \"new\" games if there  are significant changes in the new version. (There's no hard and fast  rule about what \"significant\" means here, but generally we're looking  for a decent amount of new playable content rather than mere cosmetic  upgrades.)\u003C/li>\u003Cli>Multi-game compilations, expansion packs/DLC, and Definitive/GOTY editions are  ineligible.\u003C/li>\u003Cli>Mobile games are also ineligible.\u003C/li>\u003C/ul>",{"id":2040,"type":1404,"title":2041,"slug":2042,"date":2043,"url":1316,"isVideo":1316,"description":2044,"tags":2045,"author":2047,"image":2048},"444c919e-80a7-4e09-b126-265787c3061d","Metacritic's also on           \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/playstation-3/the-lord-of-the-rings-war-in-the-north\">PlayStation 3\u003C/a>, \u003Ca href=\"https://www.metacritic.com/game/pc/the-lord-of-the-rings-war-in-the-north\">PC\u003C/a>\u003C/p>\u003Cp>A hack-and-slash with RPG elements, 2011's \u003Cem>War in the North\u003C/em> was the first game released following Warner Bros. Interactive's 2009 acquisition of the rights to adapt both Tolkien's books and Peter Jackson's films. (Previously, the rights to adapt the book and film franchises were held separately by Vivendi and EA, respectively.) Developed by Snowblind, the studio behind the excellent \u003Cem>Baldur's Gate: Dark Alliance\u003C/em>, the game borrowed its overall look from the films, though it used new voice actors and drew  elements from the books that were not in the movie trilogya€\"but it  didn't really share a focus with either, instead depicting a side quest taken on by a trio of playable characters: a ranger, a dwarf, and an elf, who can be played separately or all at once via online co-op. \u003C/p>\u003Cp>Unlike some of the \u003Cem>LOTR\u003C/em> games already discussed, \u003Cem>North\u003C/em> was aimed at older audiences, but that maturity didn't necessarily translate to a better game. Some critics liked the co-op gameplay, but many complained about the poor AI and lack of originality, and quite a few reviewers felt that \u003Cem>North\u003C/em> wore out its welcome after just a few hours.\u003C/p>\u003Cp>a€?Lord of the Rings: War in the North is fine for a few hours of jolly good fun, but once you realise that the game has played its hand early and really has nothing else to offer then the lustre is soon lost. Poor AI, boring combat and pointless quests all add up to a disappointing game that would be better off dropped into the fires of Mordor.a€? a€\"Xbox Step into a world of discovery, exploration and adventure in The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild. Travel across fields, through forests and to mountain peaks as you discover what has become of the ruined kingdom of Hyrule in this stunning open-air adventure. Explore the wilds of Hyrule any way you like - Climb up towers and mountain peaks in search of new destinations, then set your own path to get there and plunge into the wilderness. Along the way, you'll battle towering enemies, hunt wild beasts and gather ingredients for the food and elixirs you'll need to sustain you on your journey. More than 100 Shrines of Trials to discover and explore - Shrines dot the landscape, waiting to be discovered in any order you want. Search for them in various ways, and solve a variety of puzzles inside. Work your way through the traps and devices inside to earn special items and other rewards that will help you on your adventure. Be prepared and properly equipped - With an entire world waiting to be explored, you'll need a variety of outfits and gear to reach every corner. You may need to bundle up with warmer clothes or change into something better suited to the desert heat. Some clothing even has special effects that, for example, can make you faster and stealthier. The legend continues on the surface of a vast and mysterious sea as Link sets sail in his most epic, awe-inspiring adventure yet. Aided by a magical conductor's baton called the Wind Waker, he will face unimaginable monsters, explore puzzling dungeons, and meet a cast of unforgettable characters as he searches for his kidnapped sister.\n\n* Immerse yourself in a world unlike you've ever seen, as you sail across a vast, unexplored ocean.\n* Master a magical conductor's baton known as the Wind Waker to control the wind and much more! \n* Connect to a pal's Game Boy? Advance and play cooperatively -- get hints, uncover secrets, and get help in battle! \n* Use new items like the Grappling Hook and Deku Leaf to take the fight to spectacular, massive bosses. \n* Take on hordes of incredibly animated enemies as you search through riddle-filled dungeons.",{"videoClosestPastAvailableDate":1316,"videoClosestFutureAvailableDate":1316,"videoClosestPastExpiryDate":1316,"videoClosestFutureExpiryDate":1316,"videoClosestPastAvailableNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestFutureAvailableNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestPastExpiryNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestFutureExpiryNetworkId":1316,"networkStreamingDates":1316},{"score":851},{"id":2445,"type":1292,"typeId":1293,"title":2446,"slug":2447,"premiereYear":2448,"releaseDate":2449,"rating":1347,"seoUrl":1316,"image":2450,"criticScoreSummary":2454,"genres":2458,"numberOfSeasons":15,"network":1316,"description":2460,"duration":15,"streamingDates":2461,"userScore":2462},1300018488,"Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas","grand-theft-auto-san-andreas",2004,"2004-10-26",{"id":1316,"filename":2451,"dateCreated":2452,"alt":1316,"credits":1316,"path":1316,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1316,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1111,"height":2392,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1324,"bucketPath":2453,"mediaType":1316,"provider":1316},"6-1-53856-13.jpg",{"date":1316,"timezone":1316},"/provider/6/3/6-1-53856-13.jpg",{"url":2455,"max":38,"score":2456,"reviewCount":2438,"positiveCount":2457,"neutralCount":1120,"negativeCount":15,"sentiment":1305},"/game/grand-theft-auto-san-andreas/critic-reviews/",95,79,[2459],{"id":1316,"name":1346},"[Metacritic's 2004 PS2 Game of the Year]  Five years ago Carl Johnson escaped from the pressures of life in Los Santos, San Andreas... a city tearing itself apart with gang trouble, drugs and corruption. Where filmstars and millionaires do their best to avoid the dealers and gangbangers. Now, it's the early 90s. Carl's got to go home. His mother has been murdered, his family has fallen apart and his childhood friends are all heading towards disaster. On his return to the neighborhood, a couple of corrupt cops frame him for homicide. CJ is forced on a journey that takes him across the entire state of San Andreas, to save his family and to take control of the streets. Welcome to the 1980s. Having just made it back onto the streets of Liberty City after a long stretch in maximum security, Tommy Vercetti is sent to Vice City by his old boss, Sonny Forelli. They were understandably nervous about his re-appearance in Liberty City, so a trip down south seemed like a good idea. But all does not go smoothly upon his arrival in the glamorous, hedonistic metropolis of Vice City. He's set up and is left with no money and no merchandise. His only answer is to fight back and take over the city himself. Vice City offers vehicular pleasures to suit every taste. For the speed enthusiast, there's high-performance cars and motorbikes. For the sportsman, a powerboat or a golf buggy lets you enjoy the great outdoors. This game tells the epic story of Hyrule and Link?, Zelda?, and Ganondorf in remarkable depth and gives context to the many tales of their struggles. Phenomenally Improved Graphics Beyond even the incredible 3D effect, every graphic texture and character model has been lovingly built from the ground up to create the most dynamic and vivid vision of Zelda¡¯s kingdom yet. This is Hyrule as you¡¯ve always imagined it. Playing the Ocarina Link needs to play the eponymous Ocarina of Time many, many times over the course of the game. This involves a specific series of button presses to play one of the twelve songs in the game. In the original title, you would need to assign the ocarina to an item slot, then likely review the button prompts to your song, then play them from memory. The Nintendo 3DS version has the ocarina constantly available on the Touch Screen, and playing with the button prompts for each song right in front of you. The introduction of full motion controls enabled by Wii MotionPlus technology synchronizes player movements with Link's actions while offering the most intuitive play control of any game in the series to date.\r\n\r\nFEATURES:\r\n\r\n    * The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword boasts the most realistic sword combat of any game to date, turning every encounter into a challenge of both brains and brawn. Wii MotionPlus technology accurately translates the angle of every sword slice, effectively putting Link's blade in the player's hand. Analyzing enemy attack and defense tendencies and counteracting with precise swings of the sword provide a satisfying experience that blends puzzle-solving with a new and distinctly unique combat system.\r\n\r\n    * Explore a massive, multilayered world that seamlessly blends soaring exploration in a sea of clouds with intense ground-based adventuring that blurs the line between the main world and traditional Zelda dungeons. Link soars through the skies on the back of a noble bird and dives beneath the clouds to the continent below in an epic quest that lays the foundation for the events in the critically acclaimed The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time.\r\n\r\n    * Prepare for intense puzzle-solving as only The Legend of Zelda games can deliver. Exploration of the forests, volcanoes and deserts requires players to think on their feet as they solve puzzles, overcome obstacles and escape deadly traps. Once they find their way to each dungeon, they are confronted by even more puzzles guarded by fiendish enemies.\r\n\r\n    * The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword redefines the traditional Zelda framework and introduces an arsenal of items, upgrades and rewards unlike any game in the franchise to date. The adventure guides players into, out of and back to dungeons and areas they've already completed. Along the way, Link gathers a massive inventory of peculiar items and resources that can be used to upgrade many of his weapons and equipment.\r\n    * Every copy in the initial production of the game will come packaged with a special music CD featuring orchestral arrangements of select songs that will be performed at The Legend of Zelda 25th Anniversary Symphony Concert.\r\n    * This version of The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword comes packaged with a special Gold Wii Remote Plus controller. You can also purchase a copy of the game without a controller.",{"videoClosestPastAvailableDate":1316,"videoClosestFutureAvailableDate":1316,"videoClosestPastExpiryDate":1316,"videoClosestFutureExpiryDate":1316,"videoClosestPastAvailableNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestFutureAvailableNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestPastExpiryNetworkId":1316,"videoClosestFutureExpiryNetworkId":1316,"networkStreamingDates":1316},{"score":2331},{"id":2692,"type":1292,"typeId":1293,"title":2693,"slug":2694,"premiereYear":2429,"releaseDate":2695,"rating":2296,"seoUrl":1316,"image":2696,"criticScoreSummary":2700,"genres":2704,"numberOfSeasons":15,"network":1316,"description":2706,"duration":15,"streamingDates":2707,"userScore":2708},1300291120,"The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time / Master Quest","the-legend-of-zelda-ocarina-of-time-master-quest-2002","2003-02-28",{"id":1316,"filename":2697,"dateCreated":2698,"alt":1316,"credits":1316,"path":1316,"cropGravity":1316,"crop":1316,"caption":1316,"typeName":1316,"imageUrl":1316,"width":1111,"height":2392,"sType":1316,"bucketType":1324,"bucketPath":2699,"mediaType":1316,"provider":1316},"6-1-4775-13.jpg",{"date":1316,"timezone":1316},"/provider/6/3/6-1-4775-13.jpg",{"url":2701,"max":38,"score":2702,"reviewCount":2703,"positiveCount":2703,"neutralCount":15,"negativeCount":15,"sentiment":1305},"/game/the-legend-of-zelda-ocarina-of-time-master-quest-2002/critic-reviews/",91,10,[2705],{"id":1316,"name":1346},"The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Master Quest is a special version of Ocarina of Time that wasn't released for the N64 in North America. Both games were exclusively available for the Nintendo GameCube to those who preordered The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker at a participating retailer. The Master Quest version of Ocarina of Time contains the same overall storyline as the original Ocarina of Time adventure. The only exceptional aspects are the graphics and attention to detail. The story takes forever to gain momentum (10 to 15 hours), but once it does, it is a great story. Getting to that point was a serious slog, but I stuck with it because of how overrated this game is. This game is carried by its story, which is very unusual for gaming in general. The only game I can think of with a better story is the first BioShock (which was immediately captivating). Now as for the actual gameplay... that is where this game is very mediocre. The shooting is very simplistic, with a simple slow-motion (dead eye) mechanic being about the only thing to differentiate it from something like Uncharted or Tomb Raider. And there are some deeply frustrating game mechanics. For example, if you ride your horse past another NPC (on their horse or otherwise), that person will get offended that you just ran by them (and simply yell at you). But it happens even if you're like a foot away from them and don't touch them! It is seriously annoying, like the radius or hitboxes for an offense are way too big. Also, there is quite a bit of management tedium, such as: you need to brush your horse, you need to eat, you need to feed your horse, you need to drink tonics to restore health/stamina/dead eye, you need to trim your beard (it grows over time), you need to keep your horse out of camp to avoid offending others because it poops (the list goes on)... AND it is extremely difficult/finnicky to simply set up camp when you rest so that you can gain access to fast travel. 95% of the time it will say something like \"you cannot set up camp while there is activity nearby\", but you have no clue what supposed activity the game is detecting. Very annoying \"gameplay\" systems of tedium. Most of that crap is absent from the original Red Dead Redemption, which has superior gameplay, but an inferior story. I also think Days Gone has much better gameplay (but also an inferior story). Playing Red Dead Redemption 2 is more like reading a 50 hour book or TV series. It has a very slow start, annoying gameplay systems, and you'll spend about half of your time simply riding a horse and enjoying the scenery, but the story is what might make it worth enduring if you're in the right mood to just relax and take in the atmosphere of a very beautiful and detailed world.","2026-01-17","KG",1768648928631,{"id":1359,"type":2193,"title":1294,"url":2853,"platform":3462,"gameTaxonomy":3463},{"id":1356,"name":1298},{"family":3464,"title":3465,"game":3466,"platform":3467},{"id":1389,"name":1294},{"id":1291,"name":1294},{"id":1359,"name":1294},{"id":1356,"name":1298},{"id":3469,"quote":3470,"score":429,"thumbsUp":1316,"thumbsDown":1316,"date":3471,"author":3472,"version":3473,"spoiler":1030,"reviewedProduct":3474,"platform":1298},"d78c0413-23bc-457f-b4f5-2820dca5adbc","Too many fanboys saying this game is a 10, which really is impossible with all its flaws.\nFirst, your character walks (saunters) in slow  motion. That's not realistic.\nSecond, cougars don't take three bullets to the belly and then leap on your back and snap your stocky neck in three seconds.\nThird, you save a game only to realize that when you re-load it, you're spawned sometimes quite far from where you saved it. So if you want another go at that cougar scene, you've got to saunter back in slow motion through molasses and hope maybe a forth bullet to his belly will slow his nervous system or even better, make him dash away.\nFourth, a game about an outlaw in a gang of outlaws shouldn't reward honor...literally, I cannot go robbing stage coaches or trains without harming my honor. If I want to live honorably, give me an option to leave the gang and join the local church.\nThat being said, there is no open-world game out there that feels this historically alive and detailed.
